steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Start with the given equation: $p = \frac{k}{4j+9}$. The goal is to isolate $4j+9$ on one side.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $(4j+9)$ to remove it from the denominator: $p(4j+9) = k$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Divide both sides by $p$ to isolate $4j+9$: $4j+9 = \frac{k}{p}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A — $4j+9=\frac{k}{p}$: matches the derived result exactly. That confirms A is correct.
  • Choice B — $4j+9=kp$: this would mean multiplying both sides by $p$ without dividing, giving $p^2(4j+9) = kp$, which is wrong. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — $4j+9=k-p$: subtraction never appears in the algebraic manipulation. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $4j+9=\frac{p}{k}$: inverts the fraction incorrectly; numerator and denominator are swapped. Eliminated.
